briefing.opening: Welcome to the climate briefing for June 10, 2026. We will now commence.

sections.title: 🔴 Deepening Global Climate Crisis and Spreading Pessimism
sections.content: According to news released today, pessimism regarding climate crisis response is spreading within the United States, with 62% of respondents believing that the climate crisis will not be effectively addressed [2]. Notably, two-thirds of planned data center construction sites in the U.S. are already suffering from drought [3], and warnings continue that the Earth's climate balance is breaking down, making extreme weather events such as heatwaves, heavy rainfall, and typhoons difficult to predict [21]. Domestic seafood prices are also rising in succession due to the impacts of climate change and the Middle East conflict, with specific economic damages materializing, such as the wholesale price of wild flatfish surging by 37% in just one week [6, 118]. Internationally, early heatwaves have occurred in Europe, making last May the second warmest on record, and a report has been released suggesting that climate change may have caused billions of dollars in economic losses in New South Wales, Australia, escalating global concerns [127, 133].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerated Renewable Energy Transition and Market Trends
sections.content: On a positive note, investment and transition in the renewable energy sector are accelerating. Related stocks showed strong performance, with SK E&S shares hitting the upper limit amid expectations for SK Group's renewable energy business restructuring [66, 70, 98]. The establishment of a renewable energy joint venture between SK and KKR is also gaining momentum [72, 99]. The government has announced plans to invest 55GW in the solar power sector, aiming to foster it as the 'second semiconductor' [93]. Hanwha Q CELLS is advancing its global solar business, accelerating its push into both terrestrial and space renewable energy markets [108]. However, concerns have been raised that China's renewable energy manufacturing supply chain could be weaponized, emphasizing the importance of South Korea's green industry fostering strategy, 'K-GX' [80].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Domestic Carbon Neutrality Practices and Climate Adaptation Efforts
sections.content: Various domestic local governments and companies are continuing concrete efforts to respond to climate change and achieve carbon neutrality. KTC has officially commenced the development of climate change response technologies and is undertaking key technological tasks for greenhouse gas and water disaster prevention [4, 12]. Ansan City has moved to protect citizen safety amidst the impacts of climate change, and discussions are active regarding the transition to a flood response system suitable for the era of climate crisis [11, 14, 29]. Dongwon Industries is actively distributing domestically caught bluefin tuna, whose catch has increased due to climate change [8, 13]. Meanwhile, Incheon City is also implementing industrial adaptation efforts, such as releasing 1.7 million blue crabs to counter reduced catches caused by climate change [15]. Furthermore, Nowon-gu has saved 58 million won annually in its budget and practiced carbon neutrality by adjusting contracted power for public buildings [50]. In Gimhae City, a climate change-themed water park facility will open in July, introducing night operations for the first time to help citizens adapt and relax during the summer, showcasing real-life examples of climate adaptation [1, 7, 26].

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Change Awareness and Diverse Responses
sections.content: The international community is exploring enhanced awareness and diverse response strategies regarding climate change. New research indicates a link between climate change, child stunting, and regional inequality, revealing the severe impact of climate change on vulnerable populations [121]. A study showing plants migrating to higher altitudes due to climate change illustrates a facet of ecosystem change [123]. Bulgaria has expressed difficulties in resource management, warning that fixed water allocations are impossible due to climate change and refusing long-term water allocations to Greece [135]. Turkey is pursuing an energy strategy to combat climate change, aiming to increase its share of global electricity production by 35% by 2035 [132]. Meanwhile, efforts to foster public empathy and understanding are continuing, such as the CCCL Festival providing an opportunity to witness climate change through human stories rather than just headlines [134].

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has highlighted both the severity of the climate crisis and global efforts to address it, particularly the positive transition trends in the renewable energy sector. We must continue to overcome the challenges of climate change through sustained attention and effort. This concludes today's climate briefing.
